items.d: Someone whose job is to keep and look after the money of a company.
items.s: She has a job in finance. She's an ___.
items.hintDe: Diese Person kümmert sich um die Zahlen und das Geld einer Firma.

items.d: Someone whose job is at the desk in a hotel, welcoming the people who come in.
items.s: The ___ at the hotel gave us our key and a map of the city.
items.hintDe: Diese Person sitzt am Empfang im Hotel und begrüßt die Gäste.

items.d: Someone whose job is to repair machines, especially cars.
items.s: I took my car to the ___ because the engine was making a strange noise.
items.hintDe: Diese Person repariert Maschinen, vor allem Autos.

items.d: Someone whose job is to care for people who are ill, usually in a hospital.
items.s: The ___ looked after me in hospital and gave me my medicine.
items.hintDe: Diese Person pflegt kranke Menschen, meistens im Krankenhaus.

items.d: The work of looking after people who are ill, for example in hospitals.
items.s: She wants a job in ___ because she likes looking after people.
items.hintDe: Dieser Bereich kümmert sich um kranke Menschen, zum Beispiel in Krankenhäusern.

items.d: The work of showing people the things a company sells so that they want them.
items.s: She has a job in ___ and shows people why our new drink is so good.
items.hintDe: Dieser Bereich sorgt dafür, dass Leute die Produkte einer Firma kaufen wollen.

items.d: The work of looking after money for people and companies.
items.s: He wants a job in ___ because he is good with money and numbers.
items.hintDe: Dieser Bereich kümmert sich um Geld, zum Beispiel in einer Bank.

items.d: Someone whose job is to put in and repair the lights and electricity in a building.
items.s: We called an ___ to repair the broken lights in the kitchen.
items.hintDe: Diese Person repariert alles, was mit Strom zu tun hat.

items.d: Someone whose job in an office is writing letters, calling people and looking after meetings.
items.s: The ___ in our office writes the letters and looks after all the meetings.
items.hintDe: Diese Person erledigt die Büroarbeit, schreibt Briefe und plant Termine.

items.d: Someone whose job is to look after the people on a plane.
items.s: The ___ showed us how to be safe on the plane.
items.hintDe: Diese Person kümmert sich um die Passagiere im Flugzeug.

items.d: The use of computers and the work people do with them.
items.s: She wants a job in ___ because she loves making apps on the computer.
items.hintDe: In diesem Bereich arbeitet man mit Computern.

items.d: The use of computers for work, for example writing programmes.
items.s: I love programming, so I'm glad I found a job in ___.
items.hintDe: In diesem Bereich verarbeitet man Daten mit dem Computer.

items.d: The work of looking after money for a bank or a company.
items.s: She has a job in ___. She's an accountant.
items.hintDe: In diesem Bereich geht es um Geld, zum Beispiel in einer Bank.

items.d: The work of helping people who are ill, for example as a nurse or doctor.
items.s: If you want to work in ___, you need to like helping people.
items.hintDe: In diesem Bereich kümmert man sich um kranke Menschen.

items.d: The part of a company that sells things and shows people why they are good.
items.s: He is good with people. No wonder he has a job in ___.
items.hintDe: In diesem Bereich verkauft man Produkte und macht Werbung dafür.

items.d: To have a right to something good because of what you have done.
items.s: You studied so well for school — you ___ a great result.
items.hintDe: Etwas zu Recht bekommen, weil man es sich verdient hat.

items.d: A woman or a girl, not a man or a boy.
items.s: My dog's a girl, so it's ___, not male.
items.hintDe: So nennt man das Geschlecht von Frauen und Mädchen.

items.d: A man or a boy, not a woman or a girl.
items.s: My sister has a dog, and it's ___, not female.
items.hintDe: So nennt man das Geschlecht von Männern und Jungen.

items.d: The happy feeling you have when you do something well.
items.s: I love my job. It gives me a lot of ___.
items.hintDe: Das gute Gefühl, wenn du etwas geschafft hast oder zufrieden bist.

items.d: Not having a job that pays you money.
items.s: I lost my job last week. Now I'm ___.
items.hintDe: So heißt jemand, der gerade keine Arbeit hat.

items.d: The jobs a person does in one kind of work over many years.
items.s: She started her ___ as a singer ten years ago.
items.hintDe: Der berufliche Weg, den jemand über viele Jahre geht.

items.d: To like something very much or be very happy to do it.
items.s: I was very tired, so I wasn't ___ going out that evening.
items.hintDe: Etwas sehr gerne mögen oder begeistert davon sein.

items.d: To be the one who has to look after something or make sure it happens.
items.s: In our team, Tom is ___ calling the customers every morning.
items.hintDe: Derjenige sein, der sich um etwas kümmern muss.

items.d: Extra money for doing your job especially well.
items.s: Everyone in the office got a ___ for their great work this year.
items.hintDe: Extra Geld, das du für gute Arbeit bekommst.

items.d: The last day or time when your work needs to be done.
items.s: We had to hurry to meet the ___.
items.hintDe: Der letzte Zeitpunkt, bis zu dem etwas fertig sein muss.

items.d: To make something new over time, for example a new product.
items.s: The company ___ new apps for mobile phones.
items.hintDe: Etwas Neues nach und nach entstehen lassen.

items.d: To be paid money for the work that you do.
items.s: I need to ___ a lot of money if I want to go on holiday to Australia.
items.hintDe: Geld für deine Arbeit bekommen.

items.d: To bring out a new product and start selling it for the first time.
items.s: The new product will be ___ in July.
items.hintDe: Etwas Neues herausbringen und beginnen, es zu verkaufen.

items.d: The good and the bad sides of something.
items.s: Think about the ___ before you take a new job.
items.hintDe: Die guten und die schlechten Seiten von etwas.

items.d: The money you are paid for your job every month.
items.s: She earns a good ___.
items.hintDe: Das Geld, das du jeden Monat für deine Arbeit bekommst.

items.d: To make a new idea or plan in your head.
items.s: We need to ___ a good name for our new school band.
items.hintDe: Sich eine neue Idee oder einen Plan ausdenken.

items.d: The times of the day when a person is at their job.
items.s: Most people enjoy shorter ___ and more free time.
items.hintDe: Die Zeiten am Tag, an denen jemand arbeitet.

items.d: What you tell somebody to help them do the right thing.
items.s: Take my ___. Don't do it.
items.hintDe: Wenn du jemandem sagst, was er tun sollte, um zu helfen.

items.d: A strong wish to do or become something great in your life.
items.s: Her ___ is to become a famous singer one day.
items.hintDe: Ein starker Wunsch, etwas Großes zu erreichen.

items.d: Free and easy, not too smart or serious.
items.s: Don't be too ___ during an interview for a new job.
items.hintDe: Entspannt und locker, nicht zu schick oder ernst.

items.d: A business that makes or sells things and gives people jobs.
items.s: My dad has a job in a small ___ that makes furniture.
items.hintDe: Ein Betrieb, der Dinge herstellt oder verkauft.

items.d: In a way that shows you believe in yourself.
items.s: Try to speak and act ___, but don't be cheeky.
items.hintDe: Auf eine Art, die zeigt, dass du an dich selbst glaubst.

items.d: A person or company that pays other people to work for them.
items.s: My new ___ is a big computer company.
items.hintDe: Eine Person oder Firma, die andere für sich arbeiten lässt.

items.d: Showing a lot of happy and excited feelings about something.
items.s: The boys and girls were very ___ about the school trip to London.
items.hintDe: Voller Interesse und Begeisterung für etwas.

items.d: When you look right at somebody while you talk to them.
items.s: Look at your interviewer and try to keep ___.
items.hintDe: Jemandem beim Sprechen direkt in die Augen schauen.

items.d: A meeting where somebody asks you things to find out if you are right for the work.
items.s: She has an ___ next week for a new job.
items.hintDe: Ein Gespräch, in dem man dir Fragen stellt, um zu sehen, ob du zum Job passt.

items.d: To keep something well in your head so that you can use it again later.
items.s: Read the text and try to ___ it all.
items.hintDe: Sich etwas so gut merken, dass du es später auswendig kannst.

items.d: In a normal and easy way, just like you really are.
items.s: During the interview, speak ___ and just be yourself.
items.hintDe: Auf eine normale, ungezwungene Art.

items.d: The things you can do well because you have done them many times.
items.s: We need somebody with good people ___ for this job.
items.hintDe: Die Dinge, die du gut kannst, weil du sie gelernt hast.

items.d: The work of finding and writing the news for newspapers and TV programmes.
items.s: She can write well. She's looking for a career in ___.
items.hintDe: Die Arbeit, Nachrichten zu finden und aufzuschreiben.
